ELLIOTT WAVE SET UPS

SPX - WXY (August 08 Bottom) - August 2024

Before

After

This setup captured a complex, higher-degree WXY correction unfolding from the [W3] peak, where we were anticipating a 30–38.2% retracement for Wave [W4]. SPX formed a textbook 3–3–3 structure, with the Y-wave extending precisely to the 161.8% projection of W. Adding confluence there, price also held the 90-100% extensions of the fractal a | b within the Y-Wave and right above the 38.2% common 4th wave retracement. 

The projection called for [W4] to complete at that level, setting the stage for a multi-month rally in Wave [W5]. As shown in the Daily chart example, that bullish resolution played out with precision—launching from the projected low to rally nearly 1,000 points higher.

SPX - 4th/5th Wave Into WXY - August 2024

Before

After

This was one of the most precise projections of the year. In July 2024, SPX completed a [W3] top, and I forecasted a 450-point, 3-wave decline for [W4], followed by a 1,000-point rally in [W5]. The projection nailed every aspect—structure, levels, timing, and targets—with the August low marking the exact bottom of the [W4] zone before launching into the final leg. This setup showcases the power of combining higher-degree wave analysis with clean Fibonacci symmetry.

SPX - 5 Wave Down To ABC - July 2024

Before

After

SPX completed a 5-wave decline, then rallied to the 50% retracement of the move—likely forming a 2nd or B-wave. The 50% retracement level offered added confluence, aligning with A=C (90-100%) equality of the internal leg within the corrective rally. The projection called for a continuation lower toward the measured move equal to the initial 5-wave decline, which was successfully reached.

NQ - Impulse Down With WXY Up - October 2024

Before

After

NQ completed a clean 5-wave decline, followed by a textbook bearish WXY correction. With the higher time frame bias already bearish, confidence was high that the structure would resolve lower. The projection called for a break below 50% of the Y-wave to confirm the WXY pattern, triggering a measured move toward the 90–100% extensions of the initial impulse. Price followed through precisely, hitting the projected downside targets.

SPX - 2/4 Trendline - December 2024

Before

After

This was a textbook example of the 2–4 trendline model. After price broke the trendline connecting the Wave 2 and Wave 4 lows—without overlapping any internal price swings—it retraced 61.8–78.6% of the decline from the 5th wave high. Since the 5th wave high should not be crossed, the projection called for rejection from this retracement zone and continuation to the downside. The setup played out cleanly, with price declining over 50 points following the rejection.

ES - 0/B Trendline - January 2024

Before

After

ES completed an ABC down and established a valid 0-B trendline. After tapping support(orange), price crossed the 0-B trendline  suggesting a 3-wave structure to the common 50-61.8% retracements of the ABC down for a 2nd or X-Wave. The projection favored a move to the 50–61.8% retracements of the ABC decline, with confluence from the equality of the initial leg. Price followed through exactly as forecasted.

SMH - W4/W5 - January 2024

Before

After

SMH retraced 30-38.2% of the fractal 3rd wave with a 3-wave structure for a potential 4th. Projections suggested another leg up for the fractal 5th wave targeting the 50-61.8% extensions, given that the 1st wave was larger/extended. This implied the 5th wave should be shorter than the 3rd. Price responded immediately and rallied precisely into the projected target zone.

SPX - WXY - May 2024

Before

After

Price produced a neutral 3-wave move down from the high followed by a bearish WXY correction to the 61.8-78.6% retracements of the 3-wave move down. After retracing 50% of the internal X-Wave (not labeled), the bearish WXY structure was confirmed, favoring at least a cross of the prior low. Measured targets projected a 60-90 point decline - and price responded immediately, reaching both the projected zone and lower support precisely as forecasted.

SPX - Triangle Continuation - May 2024

Before

After

At the time of this projection, SPX was viewed as forming a triangle within a higher-degree Wave 3, offering a bullish bias. Upon review, the triangle more accurately completed a 4th wave relative to the May 2, 2024 swing low—implying the move that followed was a higher-degree 5th wave, which would have raised the upside targets. Despite the mislabeling, the setup still played out cleanly: price traced out a textbook A–B–C–D–E triangle, validated by holding the A-wave low, and rallied 60 points directly into the 61.8% extension of the originally labeled 3rd wave.

SPX - 3-Wave - August 2024

Before

After

Projections favored a rejection from the 50–61.8% retracement zone, which aligned with the 90–100% extensions of the A-wave—supporting the completion of Wave C. While the overall structure was complex, the bias remained clear: a move toward the lower boundary of the corrective parallel channel. This target also aligned with the 50–61.8% retracements of the broader corrective rally, adding further confluence. The move played out exactly as projected, confirming the completion of the initial trend.

NQ - Impulse Down To Corrective - October 2024

Before

After

This is a great example of how to effectively use 5-wave impulse declines. The initial 5-wave move down signaled a likely corrective rally toward the prior Wave 4 extreme—potentially forming a 2nd or B-wave. While the impulse was bearish overall, a retracement is typically expected before continuation lower. That’s why I highlighted a long opportunity into the 4th wave zone, which also aligned with the common 30–38.2–50% retracements. The projection played out perfectly, with price rallying sharply into the expected resistance area.

After

NQ - Impulse Down Into WXY - October 2024

Before

After

As projected, price completed a clean correction into the defined [W4] support zone (5,300–5,070) and respected the 0.382 retracement level without violating invalidation. This confirmed the completion of Wave [W4] and marked the beginning of Wave [W5], continuing the larger 5-wave sequence off the 2022 low.

From the [W4] low, the rally unfolded impulsively — achieving the primary upside target of 6,150, corresponding to the 61.8% extension of Waves 1 + 3. The precision of the reversal at support and the strength of the follow-through validated the original bias and confirmed the market’s adherence to classic Elliott Wave structure.

📌 Outcome: Wave [W4] completed within target zone
📌 [W5] Rally: Reached and slightly exceeded 6,150+ extension
📌 Structure Maintained: Impulse intact from 2022 low to [W5] high

This case highlights how a properly defined Wave 4 correction can provide high-conviction setups for terminal 5th wave rallies—especially when aligned with Fibonacci retracements, structure, and channel boundaries.

This confirmed the completion of Wave [W4] and marked the beginning of Wave [W5], continuing the larger 5-wave sequence off the 2022 low.

From the [W4] low, the rally unfolded impulsively — achieving the primary upside target of 6,150, corresponding to the 61.8% extension of Waves 1 + 3. The precision of the reversal at support and the strength of the follow-through validated the original bias and confirmed the market’s adherence to classic Elliott Wave structure.

📌 Structure Maintained: Impulse intact from 2022 low to [W5] high
